zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Jermyn於1424年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可盧申布盧可時，她的父親Sir Thomas Jermyn II21歲，母親Lady Margaret Leyman19歲。她於大約1441年與Baron John Bowrey Barnes在英國格盧斯特斯爾金也斯 斯坦利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1470年6月1日在英國素福爾可盧申布盧可享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Jermyn于1424年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可盧申布盧可时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Jermyn II21岁，母亲Lady Margaret Leyman19岁。她于大约1441年与Baron John Bowrey Barnes在英国格盧斯特斯爾金也斯 斯坦利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1470年6月1日在英国素福爾可盧申布盧可享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Jermyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   German   or   Jeremy  .

variants.name: German
meanings: English: ethnic name from Old French germain ‘German’ (from Latin Germanus). This sometimes denoted an actual immigrant from Germany, but was also used to refer to a person who had trade or other connections with German-speaking lands. The Latin word Germanus is of obscure and disputed origin; the most plausible of the etymologies that have been proposed is that the people were originally known as the ‘spear-men’, with ancient Germanic gēr, gār ‘spear’ as the first element.

Spanish and Hungarian (Germán); English (of Norman origin) and German: from the personal name Spanish and Hungarian Germán, Old French Germain, German German, from Latin Germanus (see Germain ), a Christian saint's name.
Americanized form of German   Germann   1, a name of ancient Germanic origin.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Jermyn was born in 1424, in Rushbrooke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Thomas Jermyn II, was 21 and her mother, Lady Margaret Leyman, was 19. She married Baron John Bowrey Barnes about 1441, in King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 1 June 1470, in Rushbrooke, Suffolk, England, at the age of 46.

Elle a épousé Baron John Bowrey Barnes environ 1441, à King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1470, à Rushbrooke, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans.

ko.Barking and Dagenham, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 바킨그 앤드 다겐함
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Jermyn(이)가 1424년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 러쉬브룩에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Jermyn II(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Leyman(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1441년 영국 그루스스터실르 킨그'스 스탠리에서 Baron John Bowrey Barnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1470년 6월 1일 영국 수폴크 러쉬브룩에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Barking and Dagenham, London, England: баркинг анд дадженам, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Jermyn родилась в 1424 г. в местности рушбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Jermyn II было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Leyman, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baron John Bowrey Barnes около 1441 г. в местности кинг'с стэнли, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 1 июня 1470 г. в местности рушбрук, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 46 лет.
